metadata.summary: Production patterns for reliable LLM structured extraction: Pydantic schema design, retry with error-message injection, streaming JSON handling, and the StructuredOutputService abstraction.

content.title: Structured Output Patterns
content.summary: Production patterns for reliable LLM structured extraction: Pydantic schema design, retry with error-message injection, streaming JSON handling, and the StructuredOutputService abstraction. Core rule: keep schemas flat (1–2 nesting levels) — models handle flat schemas with near-zero errors; deeply nested schemas (5+ levels) cause frequent parse failures even in structured output mode.

why.p: Ad-hoc json.loads() after LLM calls fails silently on missing fields, wrong types, and validation errors. A service layer with Pydantic validation, typed retry, and structured fallback ensures type safety at every pipeline handoff and prevents bad data from propagating downstream.

list.item: Agent pipelines passing data between steps — structured output prevents parse errors at handoff points
Extracting information from unstructured text (emails, docs, PDFs) for downstream processing
Multi-agent coordination where subagent outputs must conform to a contract
Any agent output feeding into a database, API, or service expecting typed data
Replacing regex-based parsing with LLM extraction for complex, variable-format inputs
Free-form conversational responses — forcing JSON on chat output degrades quality
Schema changes frequently — Pydantic + re-generation overhead is unnecessary if shape unknown at design time
Simple string outputs (yes/no, short answers) — structured output adds token overhead for no gain
OpenAI Structured Outputs beta unavailable for your model tier — fall back to JSON mode with manual validation

rule.statement: Keep schemas flat (1–2 levels of nesting); split deeply nested schemas into multiple sequential extraction calls rather than one complex schema.
rule.rationale: Models handle flat schemas with near-zero errors. 5+ nesting levels cause frequent partial or malformed outputs even in structured output mode. Each additional level of nesting reduces reliability. Complex nested schemas (5+ levels) confuse models and produce partial or malformed outputs even in structured output mode.

rule.statement: Use Literal["a", "b", "c"] instead of str for enumerated fields — it is a hard constraint enforced by the schema, not a soft instruction that the model may ignore.
rule.rationale: Telling the model "use one of: success, partial, failed" in a description is a soft instruction. Literal enforces it at schema validation time, catching typos and hallucinated values.

rule.statement: Add Field(description="...") to all non-obvious fields — the description is injected into the schema and guides the model during generation.
rule.rationale: Without descriptions, the model guesses field semantics from the name alone. Ambiguous field names (e.g., "value", "data", "result") produce inconsistent content without descriptions.

example.title: Flat, typed agent output schema
example.code:
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field from typing import Optional, Literal, List
class AgentTaskResult(BaseModel): status: Literal["success", "partial", "failed"] output: str = Field(description="Primary result text, max 500 chars") confidence: float = Field(ge=0.0, le=1.0, description="0.0=no confidence, 1.0=certain") missing_data: List[str] = Field( default_factory=list, description="Fields the agent could not extract; empty list if all found") next_action: Optional[str] = Field( default=None, description="Recommended follow-up action, or null if complete")

rule.statement: Treat Pydantic models as API contracts and version them (v1, v2); schema changes break existing extraction without a version bump, and old cached responses will fail validation.
rule.rationale: Schema changes silently break agents that cached the old schema. Versioned models make incompatibilities explicit and allow graceful migration.

antipattern.description: Declaring Optional[X] without a default value (e.g., field: Optional[str]).
antipattern.reason: Optional without a default still makes the field required at Pydantic instantiation, causing ValidationError on LLM output that omits the field. Always use Optional[X] = None or Field(default=None).

section.name: Retry Logic, Streaming, and StructuredOutputService

rule.statement: On ValidationError retry, include the error message in the next prompt — blind retries rarely improve results because the model repeats the same mistake without knowing what failed.
rule.rationale: The model does not know it failed validation unless told. Adding the Pydantic error message to the prompt ("The previous response failed validation: {error}. Please fix: ...") guides it to correct the specific field. When a structured extraction fails after max_retries, the agent should log the raw LLM output and return None, not crash.

rule.statement: Log all ValidationError instances with the raw LLM output — patterns in failures reveal schema improvement opportunities.
rule.rationale: Repeated failures on the same field indicate the schema description is inadequate or the field is genuinely ambiguous. Logs are the only way to identify and fix these patterns. Pydantic ValidationError on LLM output is not always recoverable by retrying — sometimes the schema is genuinely too complex for the model.

rule.statement: Do not parse partial JSON chunks from a streaming structured output call — partial JSON is not valid JSON and json.loads() will fail on every chunk except the last.
rule.rationale: Streaming + JSON mode produces one valid JSON only at completion. Attempting to parse intermediary chunks wastes compute and produces only errors. Stream for UX progress indicators only, not for parsing. Streaming JSON parsing is fragile; partial JSON is not valid JSON and json.loads will fail on every chunk except the last.

rule.statement: Centralize structured extraction in a StructuredOutputService that handles retries, schema injection, and validation; subagents receive typed Pydantic objects as inputs and outputs, not raw strings.
rule.rationale: Duplicating extraction logic across subagents creates inconsistent error handling and retry behavior. A shared service ensures uniform behavior across the pipeline. Define output schemas as Pydantic models in a shared schemas.py module and pass them to a StructuredOutputService instance. The service handles retries, schema injection, and validation. Subagents receive typed Pydantic objects as inputs and outputs — not raw strings — ensuring type safety at every pipeline stage.

section.note: Recommended subagents: faion-sdd-executor-agent receives structured task specs as Pydantic objects, enforcing the SDD contract. Any extraction subagent that reads documents and emits typed data for storage. Always validate with Pydantic — never json.loads() + manual field access without type checking.

section.name: Force JSON Extraction via System Prompt

example.title: JSON mode system prompt pattern

section.name: OpenAI Structured Outputs

example.title: Native structured outputs (no schema in prompt)
example.code: response = client.beta.chat.completions.parse( model="gpt-4o", messages=[{"role": "user", "content": text}], response_format=MyPydanticModel ) result = response.choices[0].message.parsed # already validated Pydantic instance

section.note: response.choices[0].message.parsed is None if the model produced invalid JSON even in Structured Outputs mode — always null-check. OpenAI Structured Outputs (response_format=OutputClass) is only available for gpt-4o and newer — older models need JSON mode fallback.

section.name: CLI Tools and Libraries
section.text: pydantic — Schema definition and validation (pip install pydantic / https://docs.pydantic.dev) openai (Python SDK) — Structured outputs and JSON mode (pip install openai) anthropic (Python SDK) — Claude tool-use as structured output (pip install anthropic) tenacity — Retry on ValidationError / JSONDecodeError (pip install tenacity) instructor — Thin wrapper for structured outputs across providers (pip install instructor / https://python.useinstructor.com) For bulk extraction, use OpenAI Batch API — 50% cost reduction, async 24h window.

list.item: Keep schemas flat (1–2 levels of nesting) — models handle flat schemas with near-zero errors
Use Literal["a", "b", "c"] instead of str for enumerated fields — hard constraint, not soft instruction
Add Field(description="...") to all non-obvious fields — the description is injected into the schema and guides the model
Always validate with Pydantic — never json.loads() + manual field access without type checking
Use instructor library if you need cross-provider structured output without duplicating logic
Very large schemas inflate system prompt token usage; schemas over ~2K tokens should be split across multiple extraction calls
Claude tool-use for structured output returns block.input as a dict, not a Pydantic model — validate it manually: MyModel(**block.input)
Very large output schemas can cause the model to produce only the first few fields and stop at max_tokens — increase max_tokens or split the schema

template.purpose: StructuredOutputService with retry, parse fallback, and batch_extract
